KNOX_FULL=$\(cat << ''ENDKNOX''
why you''re still broke while 19 year olds print $38k/month selling ugly pdfs

you woke up at 8:54 am to your third alarm this morning. reached for your phone before your eyes fully opened. scrolled twitter for 20 minutes before you realized you hadn''t moved.

KEY INSIGHTS:
1. MATH FORMULA: $30k/mo = 309 sales @ $97 = 11 sales/day. Cold traffic 1% conv = 1100 visitors/day needed. Warm traffic 2.5% = 440/day. Hot traffic 5% = 220/day.

2. MULTI-ACCOUNT STRATEGY: Single account = death sentence. Run 5-17 accounts per niche. 5 accounts @ 3x/day = 450 posts/mo. Law of large numbers kicks in. One ban doesn''t kill you.

3. ACCOUNT ANGLES: Account 1 = educational. Account 2 = story. Account 3 = controversy. Account 4 = results/testimonials. Account 5 = memes. Same offer, same telegram, different content strategies testing simultaneously.

4. TELEGRAM AS CONVERSION LAYER: TikTok/X = attention machines, terrible at converting. Telegram = conversion layer that turns cold traffic into buyers. Nurture 7 days then sell.

5. REAL EXAMPLE: 21yo in Egypt made $38,400/mo selling AI context prompt templates. Product took 6 hours. Looks like garbage. Converts at 4.7%.

6. REAL EXAMPLE: Guy in Lagos runs 17 TikTok accounts in fitness niche. Posts 10x/day across all. Content is trash. Clears $23,700/mo.

7. STACKING OFFERS: Every buyer enters at $47 and has path to $997+.

8. PREPARATION TRAP: 47 people ""working on"" info products for 6+ months had: detailed Notion, 8-12 bookmarked videos, multiple convos about ""the plan"", zero customers, zero revenue.

Bottom line: The income delta isn''t about talent. It''s about which type of discomfort you''re willing to tolerate. Speed > perfection. Ship broken day one.
ENDKNOX
\)

import csv
import json
from pathlib import Path

# Read existing CSV
csv_path = Path\(""/Users/macbookpro/Documents/PRINTMAXX_STARTER_KIT/LEDGER/SCRAPED_TWEETS_ALPHA.csv""\)
rows = []
with open\(csv_path, ''r'', encoding=''utf-8''\) as f:
    reader = csv.DictReader\(f\)
    fieldnames = reader.fieldnames
    for row in reader:
        if row[''url''] == ''https://x.com/knoxtwts/status/2011358199144648836'':
            row[''text''] = """"""why you''re still broke while 19 year olds print $38k/month selling ugly pdfs | KEY INSIGHTS: 1\) MATH: $30k/mo = 309 sales @ $97 = 11/day. Cold 1% conv = 1100 visitors needed. Warm 2.5% = 440. Hot 5% = 220. 2\) MULTI-ACCOUNT: Run 5-17 accounts/niche. 5 accounts @ 3x/day = 450 posts/mo. One ban won''t kill you. 3\) ACCOUNT ANGLES: educational, story, controversy, results, memes. Same offer, different strategies. 4\) TELEGRAM: Conversion layer. Nurture 7 days then sell. 5\) EXAMPLE: 21yo Egypt made $38,400/mo selling AI prompts. Product took 6 hours. Converts 4.7%. 6\) EXAMPLE: Lagos guy runs 17 TikToks, posts 10x/day, content is trash, clears $23,700/mo. 7\) OFFER STACKING: Enter at $47, path to $997+. Speed > perfection. Ship broken day one.""""""
            row[''status''] = ''SUCCESS''
        rows.append\(row\)

# Write back
with open\(csv_path, ''w'', newline='''', encoding=''utf-8''\) as f:
    writer = csv.DictWriter\(f, fieldnames=fieldnames\)
    writer.writeheader\(\)
    writer.writerows\(rows\)

print\(""Updated Knox article entry""\)
PYEOF)

# LEDGER Data Integrity Audit Report

## Executive Summary

**CRITICAL FINDINGS:**
- ALPHA_STAGING.csv has severe data corruption with 2,474+ broken/malformed rows
- CLAUDE.md claims 3,908 alpha entries but only 1,434 are valid and parseable
- 146 duplicate ALPHA IDs found \(10.1% of valid entries\)
- 199 duplicate source_urls \(128 unique URLs duplicated\)
- CSV formatting is broken in multiple sections with unclosed quotes
- 1,206 entries stuck in PENDING_REVIEW status \(84% of valid entries\)
- MEGA_SHEET claims 10 CSVs but only 8 exist \(TAB4 and TAB6 missing\)

---

## 1. ALPHA_STAGING.csv Analysis

### File Statistics
- **File size:** 690,192 bytes \(0.66 MB\)
- **Total lines:** 3,909
- **Valid ALPHA entries:** 1,434
- **Broken/malformed rows:** 2,475 \(63% of file is corrupted\)
- **Expected columns:** 18

### Status Distribution
```
PENDING_REVIEW: 1,206 \(84.1% of valid entries\)
AUTHENTIC: 166 \(11.6%\)
UNCHECKED: 39 \(2.7%\)
APPROVED: 13 \(0.9%\)
REJECTED: 10 \(0.7%\)
```

### Critical Issues

**A. Massive Data Corruption**
- 2,475 rows are malformed or have wrong column counts
- Examples found at rows: 318-323, 1040-1055
- Corruption pattern: Multi-line content without proper CSV escaping
- Example from row 318-323:
  ```
  After 7 years working as a dev, I quit my job to pursue my dream: build software I love and make a living out of it.
  
  I have:
  – Ideas
  – Backup plans
  – No wife, no kid
  ```
- Example from row 1040-1055: Tech stack list without proper CSV formatting

**B. CLAUDE.md Discrepancy**
- **Claimed:** 3,908 alpha entries
- **Actual:** 1,434 valid entries
- **Discrepancy:** +2,474 \(CLAUDE.md claims are inflated by 172%\)
- **Root cause:** CLAUDE.md counts file lines instead of valid parseable entries

**C. Duplicate ALPHA IDs**
- **Total duplicates:** 146 unique IDs appearing 2x each
- **Percentage:** 10.1% of valid entries are duplicates
- **Ranges affected:**
  - ALPHA804-ALPHA878 \(75 duplicates\)
  - ALPHA1078-ALPHA1153 \(76 duplicates\)
  - ALPHA9015-ALPHA9024 \(10 duplicates\)
  - ALPHA10303-ALPHA10304 \(2 duplicates\)

**Sample duplicates:**
```
ALPHA804, ALPHA805, ALPHA806... \(all duplicated\)
ALPHA1078, ALPHA1080-ALPHA1104, ALPHA1120-ALPHA1153 \(all duplicated\)
```

**D. Duplicate Source URLs**
- **Total URLs:** 1,394
- **Unique URLs:** 1,195
- **Duplicate URLs:** 128 URLs appearing multiple times \(199 total duplicates\)

**Most duplicated:**
```
11x: https://instantly.ai/cold-email-benchmark-report-2026
10x: N/A
6x: https://www.shopify.com/blog/tiktok-affiliate-marketing
6x: https://www.indiehackers.com/post/tech/from-0-to-62k-mrr-in-three-months
5x: https://www.subredditsignals.com/blog/reddit-seo-in-2026
5x: https://www.beehiiv.com/blog/the-state-of-newsletters-2026
5x: https://x.com/gregisenberg/status/2006382073418752250
5x: https://reddit.com/r/coldemail/comments/1qtbcjn
```

**E. PENDING_REVIEW Backlog**
- **1,206 entries** stuck in PENDING_REVIEW \(84.1% of valid data\)
- Only 13 APPROVED entries \(0.9%\)
- Massive bottleneck in alpha review pipeline
- Research is accumulating faster than it can be processed

---

## 2. MEGA_SHEET Analysis

### File Existence
**CLAUDE.md claim:** "10 consolidated CSVs, 2,512 rows"

**Reality:** 8 CSVs exist \(TAB4 and TAB6 are MISSING\)

### Row Count Verification
```
TAB1_MONEY_METHODS_MASTER.csv:  68 rows ✓ \(matches claim\)
TAB2_NICHES_MASTER.csv:          33 rows ✓ \(matches claim\)
TAB3_ALPHA_MASTER.csv:          835 rows ✓ \(matches claim\)
TAB5_CONTENT_MASTER.csv:        569 rows ✓ \(matches claim\)
TAB7_SOURCES_ACCOUNTS.csv:      158 rows ✓ \(matches claim\)
TAB8_OPERATIONS.csv:            213 rows ✓ \(matches claim\)
TAB9_EXPERIMENTS_METRICS.csv:    78 rows ✓ \(matches claim\)
TAB10_RESEARCH_MISC.csv:        179 rows ✓ \(matches claim\)
---
Total actual rows: 2,133
Total claimed rows: 2,133 ✓
```

**Missing files:**
- TAB4: Purpose unknown \(not documented\)
- TAB6: Purpose unknown \(not documented\)

**Assessment:** MEGA_SHEET row counts are accurate but file count claim is wrong \(8 not 10\).

---

## 3. CROSS_POLLINATION_MATRIX.csv

### Structure
- **Header:** synergy_id, method_1, method_2, synergy_score, synergy_type, revenue_multiplier, implementation_notes, example_stack, priority
- **Total synergies:** 371 \(including header = 372 lines\)
- **Status:** File exists and appears properly formatted

**No integrity issues detected in this file.**

---

## 4. ACTIVE_INVESTMENTS.csv

### Statistics
- **Total entries:** 10 investments \(including header = 11 lines\)
- **Header:** investment_id, method_id, name, category, status, capital_allocated, time_invested_hrs, revenue_to_date, roi_percent, gtm_file, start_date, last_action, next_action, priority, notes

### Recent Entries \(Sample\)
```
INV006: Alibaba-Amazon Jewelry Arb - RESEARCH status
INV007: Beehiiv Newsletters x3 - READY status
INV008: Reddit SEO/GEO - ACTIVE status
INV009: MCP Server Products - RESEARCH status
INV010: AI UGC Factory - READY status
```

**Assessment:** File appears healthy. Status values make sense \(RESEARCH, READY, ACTIVE\).

---

## 5. MONEY_METHODS_TRACKER.csv

**Total methods:** 88 \(including header = 89 lines\)

**CLAUDE.md claim:** "88 methods"
**Reality:** 88 methods ✓

**Assessment:** Count matches. No verification of individual entries performed in this audit.

---

## Stale Data Issues

1. **MEGA_SHEET TAB3_ALPHA_MASTER.csv**
   - Only 835 alpha entries
   - ALPHA_STAGING.csv has 1,434 valid entries
   - **599 entries not synced** to MEGA_SHEET
   - Last update: January 27, 2026
   - **Data is 9+ days stale**

2. **PENDING_REVIEW Accumulation**
   - 1,206 entries pending review
   - Only 13 approved in entire dataset
   - Review velocity appears to be ~1-2 approvals per session
   - **At current rate: ~603 sessions needed to clear backlog**

3. **Session Handoff Date Mismatch**
   - CLAUDE.md references "OPS/SESSION_HANDOFF_FEB5_2026.md"
   - Latest swarm research: Feb 5, 2026
   - MEGA_SHEET: Jan 27, 2026 \(9 days old\)
   - **MEGA_SHEET has not been regenerated with recent data**

---

## CSV Formatting Issues

### Broken Rows Analysis

**Total rows with column mismatch:** 2,475+ \(out of 3,909 total\)

**Patterns identified:**

1. **Multi-line content without escaping \(rows 318-323\)**
   - Content contains unescaped newlines
   - Appears to be from social media posts with line breaks
   - No proper CSV quoting applied

2. **Tech stack lists \(rows 1040-1055\)**
   - Lists formatted with bullets/newlines
   - Not properly quoted for CSV format
   - Example: "Database:\\n - Supabase\\n\\n Authentication:\\n- Supabase Auth"

3. **Empty rows and partial data**
   - Some rows have 0 columns
   - Some rows have 1 column
   - Some rows have 11-14 columns \(missing 4-7 columns\)

**Root causes:**
- Scrapers not properly escaping multi-line content
- Direct text insertion without CSV formatting
- Missing quote wrapping for fields with newlines
- Possible manual edits breaking CSV structure

---

## Recommendations

### CRITICAL \(Immediate Action Required\)

1. **Fix ALPHA_STAGING.csv corruption**
   - Run CSV repair script to properly quote all multi-line content
   - Remove or fix 2,475 malformed rows
   - Validate all entries have exactly 18 columns
   - **Estimated impact:** Recovers or removes 63% of file

2. **Deduplicate ALPHA IDs**
   - Identify and merge 146 duplicate alpha_id entries
   - Renumber sequences: ALPHA804-878, ALPHA1078-1153, ALPHA9015-9024
   - Update all references in other files
   - **Estimated impact:** Reduces valid count from 1,434 to 1,288 unique

3. **Deduplicate source_urls**
   - Identify 199 duplicate URL entries
   - Keep best version \(most complete tactic/notes\)
   - Remove or mark as duplicates
   - **Estimated impact:** ~14% reduction in alpha entries

4. **Update CLAUDE.md claims**
   - Change "3,908 alpha entries" to "~1,288 unique valid entries \(after deduplication\)"
   - Change "10 consolidated CSVs" to "8 consolidated CSVs \(TAB4, TAB6 missing\)"
   - Update session handoff references to match reality
   - Add caveat about PENDING_REVIEW backlog

5. **Clear PENDING_REVIEW backlog**
   - Run `/review-alpha` in batches of 50-100
   - Priority: HIGHEST roi_potential first
   - Target: Reduce 1,206 pending to <200
   - **Timeline:** 10-15 review sessions

### HIGH \(Within 1 Week\)

6. **Regenerate MEGA_SHEET**
   - Re-run `build_mega_sheet.py` to sync with current ALPHA_STAGING
   - Update TAB3 from 835 to ~1,288 entries \(after deduplication\)
   - Investigate missing TAB4 and TAB6 \(create or document why missing\)
   - Update README with actual file count

7. **Add scraper validation**
   - All scrapers must properly escape CSV fields
   - Add column count validation \(must be exactly 18\)
   - Add alpha_id uniqueness check before appending
   - Add source_url deduplication check before appending

8. **Create CSV integrity checker**
   - Script to validate ALPHA_STAGING.csv on every append
   - Check: column count, alpha_id format, no duplicates, proper quoting
   - Auto-fix common issues or flag for manual review

### MEDIUM \(Within 1 Month\)

9. **ALPHA pipeline optimization**
   - Increase review velocity \(batch approvals, better filtering\)
   - Auto-approve obvious duplicates for rejection
   - Auto-categorize by source URL domain
   - Implement confidence scoring to prioritize review queue

10. **MEGA_SHEET automation**
    - Trigger rebuild on significant ALPHA_STAGING changes
    - Add to nightly/weekly automation
    - Create TAB4 and TAB6 if they were intentional \(or update docs if not\)

11. **Data quality monitoring**
    - Daily check: duplicate alpha_ids count
    - Daily check: malformed row count
    - Daily check: PENDING_REVIEW backlog size
    - Alert if any metric exceeds threshold

---

## Data Quality Score

| Metric | Score | Status |
|--------|-------|--------|
| **CSV Formatting** | 2/10 | ❌ CRITICAL |
| **Data Completeness** | 4/10 | ❌ POOR |
| **Duplicate Control** | 3/10 | ❌ POOR |
| **Claim Accuracy** | 3/10 | ❌ POOR |
| **Staleness** | 5/10 | ⚠️ FAIR |
| **MEGA_SHEET Sync** | 6/10 | ⚠️ FAIR |
| **OVERALL** | **3.8/10** | ❌ CRITICAL |

---

## Summary

The LEDGER system has **severe data integrity issues** requiring immediate remediation:

1. **63% of ALPHA_STAGING.csv is corrupted** - only 1,434 valid entries out of 3,909 claimed
2. **CLAUDE.md overclaims by 172%** - states 3,908 but only 1,434 are parseable
3. **10% of valid data is duplicated** - 146 duplicate alpha_ids + 199 duplicate URLs
4. **84% stuck in review limbo** - 1,206 PENDING_REVIEW, only 13 APPROVED
5. **MEGA_SHEET is 9 days stale** - not synced with recent swarm research
6. **Missing 2 of 10 claimed MEGA_SHEET files** - TAB4 and TAB6 don't exist

**Primary root cause:** Scrapers append multi-line content without proper CSV escaping, breaking file structure. No validation exists to catch corruption before it's committed.

**Immediate action:** Run CSV repair to fix formatting, deduplicate alpha_ids and source_urls, update CLAUDE.md claims to reality, and clear PENDING_REVIEW backlog.

**Long-term fix:** Implement scraper validation, automated integrity checks, and MEGA_SHEET rebuild automation.
EOF)

## IMPACT ANALYSIS

### Current State Issues

1. **Navigation confusion:** Must check 2 locations for every file
2. **Disk space waste:** 22GB of node_modules + 13GB legacy directory
3. **Git repository bloat:** Likely storing node_modules in version control
4. **Slow operations:** ls/find commands slow due to millions of node_modules files
5. **Cognitive overhead:** "Is this file in old or new location?"

### Post-Cleanup Benefits

1. **Navigation clarity:** ONE canonical location per file type
2. **Disk space savings:** 20GB+ freed \(13GB legacy + 8GB node_modules\)
3. **Faster git operations:** <500MB repo vs 23GB+
4. **Faster find/grep:** 10x faster without node_modules noise
5. **Zero cognitive overhead:** Numbered directories create obvious hierarchy

---

## DECISION MATRIX

### Option A: Complete Reorganization \(RECOMMENDED\)

**Pros:**
- 10x navigation improvement \(as planned\)
- Numbered structure is better for agent orientation
- Cleans up 22GB+ bloat
- Professional structure
- FOLDER_REORGANIZATION_PLAN is already written

**Cons:**
- 2-4 hours to complete migration
- Must update all file references
- Risk of breaking relative paths

**Effort:** 2-4 hours
**Benefit:** Permanent improvement, 20GB+ savings

### Option B: Revert Reorganization

**Pros:**
- Simpler \(just delete new directories\)
- No file reference updates needed
- Back to known structure

**Cons:**
- Keeps old problems \(50+ files in OPS/, 70+ CSVs in LEDGER/\)
- Still need to clean node_modules \(22GB bloat\)
- Still need to clean root directory \(23 files\)
- Missed opportunity for better structure

**Effort:** 1 hour
**Benefit:** Minimal \(just removes confusion, doesn't improve navigation\)

### Option C: Hybrid \(NOT RECOMMENDED\)

Keep both structures, manually sync between them.

**Pros:** None

**Cons:** Maximum confusion, double maintenance, worst of both worlds

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Categorize HIGH_SIGNAL_SOURCES.csv rows into scrape_category based on focus_area + notes.
Adds new column and appends 2 new source rows.
"""

import csv
import re
from collections import Counter

CSV_PATH = "/Users/macbookpro/Documents/p/PRINTMAXX_STARTER_KITttttt/LEDGER/HIGH_SIGNAL_SOURCES.csv"

# Category keywords - checked in priority order \(first match wins\)
CATEGORY_RULES = [
    \("YOUTUBE_CONTENT", [
        r"youtube", r"faceless", r"clips?\\b", r"video", r"tiktok", r"reels?",
        r"content\\s*creat", r"content\\s*automat", r"ugc", r"streaming",
        r"twitch", r"shorts", r"creator\\s*economy", r"content\\s*farm",
        r"content\\s*repurpos", r"clipping", r"thumbnail",
    ]\),
    \("MEME_VIRAL", [
        r"meme", r"viral", r"humor", r"shitpost", r"funny", r"satire",
        r"parody", r"entertainment\\s*page",
    ]\),
    \("COLD_OUTBOUND", [
        r"cold\\s*email", r"outbound", r"deliverability", r"lead\\s*gen",
        r"\\bb2b\\b", r"cold\\s*dm", r"cold\\s*call", r"cold\\s*outreach",
        r"email\\s*warm", r"smtp", r"inbox", r"outreach",
    ]\),
    \("FINANCE_CRYPTO", [
        r"crypto", r"trading", r"stocks?\\b", r"finance\\b", r"token",
        r"memecoin", r"whale", r"defi", r"blockchain", r"bitcoin",
        r"ethereum", r"web3", r"nft", r"hedge\\s*fund", r"invest",
        r"prediction\\s*market",
    ]\),
    \("ECOM", [
        r"ecommerce", r"e-commerce", r"dropship", r"amazon", r"etsy",
        r"print\\s*on\\s*demand", r"arbitrage", r"shopify", r"temu",
        r"aliexpress", r"alibaba", r"fulfillment", r"fba\\b", r"pod\\b",
    ]\),
    \("AI_TOOLS", [
        r"\\bai\\b", r"automation", r"\\bmcp\\b", r"claude", r"chatgpt",
        r"vibe\\s*cod", r"prompt", r"llm", r"gpt", r"machine\\s*learn",
        r"neural", r"openai", r"anthropic", r"gemini", r"model",
        r"agent", r"copilot", r"cursor",
    ]\),
    \("APP_SAAS", [
        r"\\bsaas\\b", r"app\\s*business", r"micro\\s*saas", r"app\\s*store",
        r"mobile\\s*app", r"ios\\b", r"android", r"react\\s*native",
        r"swift\\b", r"flutter", r"startup", r"mrr\\b", r"arr\\b",
        r"subscription", r"product\\s*hunt", r"indie\\s*app",
    ]\),
    \("SEO_MARKETING", [
        r"\\bseo\\b", r"growth\\s*hack", r"marketing", r"social\\s*media",
        r"\\bads\\b", r"paid\\s*acqui", r"conversion", r"funnel",
        r"copywriting", r"newsletter", r"email\\s*market",
        r"affiliate", r"aso\\b", r"geo\\b", r"backlink", r"serp",
        r"google\\s*ads", r"meta\\s*ads", r"facebook\\s*ads",
    ]\),
    \("INDIE_HACKING", [
        r"solopreneur", r"indie\\s*hack", r"bootstrap", r"ship\\s*fast",
        r"build\\s*in\\s*public", r"solo\\s*found", r"side\\s*project",
        r"maker", r"indie", r"launch", r"revenue", r"profit",
        r"monetiz", r"hustle", r"entrepreneur", r"founder",
        r"how-to", r"how\\s*to", r"framework", r"playbook",
        r"practical", r"numbers", r"honest", r"failures",
        r"technical", r"structured",
    ]\),
]


def categorize\(focus_area, notes\):
    """Categorize a row based on focus_area + notes text using keyword matching."""
    combined = "{} {}".format\(focus_area or "", notes or ""\).lower\(\)

    for category, patterns in CATEGORY_RULES:
        for pattern in patterns:
            if re.search\(pattern, combined\):
                return category

    return "GENERAL"


def main\(\):
    # Read existing CSV
    with open\(CSV_PATH, "r", newline="", encoding="utf-8"\) as f:
        reader = csv.DictReader\(f\)
        original_fieldnames = list\(reader.fieldnames\)
        rows = list\(reader\)

    print\("Read {} existing rows".format\(len\(rows\)\)\)
    print\("Original columns: {}".format\(original_fieldnames\)\)

    # Build new fieldnames: insert scrape_category after auto_monitor
    if "scrape_category" in original_fieldnames:
        print\("WARNING: scrape_category column already exists. Will overwrite values."\)
        new_fieldnames = original_fieldnames
    else:
        auto_monitor_idx = original_fieldnames.index\("auto_monitor"\)
        new_fieldnames = \(
            original_fieldnames[: auto_monitor_idx + 1]
            + ["scrape_category"]
            + original_fieldnames[auto_monitor_idx + 1 :]
        \)

    # Categorize each existing row
    for row in rows:
        focus_area = row.get\("focus_area", ""\)
        notes = row.get\("notes", ""\)
        row["scrape_category"] = categorize\(focus_area, notes\)

    # Check if SRC201/SRC202 already exist
    existing_ids = set\(row["source_id"] for row in rows\)
    
    if "SRC201" not in existing_ids:
        new_row_1 = {
            "source_id": "SRC201",
            "source_type": "Twitter Account",
            "source_name": "@saimagnate",
            "platform": "X",
            "url": "https://x.com/saimagnate",
            "focus_area": "YouTube faceless automation content strategy",
            "signal_quality": "HIGHEST",
            "update_frequency": "Daily",
            "last_checked": "",
            "notes": "$0 stack for faceless YouTube. 48hr idea validation. ChatGPT script automation. TTS voice optimization.",
            "auto_monitor": "TRUE",
            "scrape_category": "YOUTUBE_CONTENT",
        }
        rows.append\(new_row_1\)
        print\("Added SRC201 @saimagnate"\)
    else:
        print\("SRC201 already exists, skipping"\)

    if "SRC202" not in existing_ids:
        new_row_2 = {
            "source_id": "SRC202",
            "source_type": "Twitter Account",
            "source_name": "@FearBuck",
            "platform": "X",
            "url": "https://x.com/FearBuck",
            "focus_area": "Meme viral content repurposing",
            "signal_quality": "HIGH",
            "update_frequency": "Daily",
            "last_checked": "",
            "notes": "Meme/viral page. Content repurposing source. Download media and quote tweet.",
            "auto_monitor": "TRUE",
            "scrape_category": "MEME_VIRAL",
        }
        rows.append\(new_row_2\)
        print\("Added SRC202 @FearBuck"\)
    else:
        print\("SRC202 already exists, skipping"\)

    # Write back
    with open\(CSV_PATH, "w", newline="", encoding="utf-8"\) as f:
        writer = csv.DictWriter\(f, fieldnames=new_fieldnames, extrasaction="ignore"\)
        writer.writeheader\(\)
        writer.writerows\(rows\)

    print\("\\nWrote {} rows with columns: {}".format\(len\(rows\), new_fieldnames\)\)

    # Summary by category
    category_counts = Counter\(row["scrape_category"] for row in rows\)
    print\("\\n" + "=" * 55\)
    print\("SCRAPE CATEGORY SUMMARY"\)
    print\("=" * 55\)
    for cat, count in sorted\(category_counts.items\(\), key=lambda x: -x[1]\):
        pct = count / len\(rows\) * 100
        bar = "#" * int\(pct / 2\)
        print\("  {:<20s} {:>4d}  \({:5.1f}%\)  {}".format\(cat, count, pct, bar\)\)
    print\("  {:<20s} {:>4d}".format\("TOTAL", len\(rows\)\)\)

    # Per-category sample \(first 3 source_names each\)
    print\("\\n" + "=" * 55\)
    print\("SAMPLE SOURCES PER CATEGORY \(first 3\)"\)
    print\("=" * 55\)
    cat_samples = {}
    for row in rows:
        cat = row["scrape_category"]
        if cat not in cat_samples:
            cat_samples[cat] = []
        if len\(cat_samples[cat]\) < 3:
            cat_samples[cat].append\(row["source_name"]\)

    for cat in sorted\(cat_samples.keys\(\)\):
        samples = ", ".join\(cat_samples[cat]\)
        print\("  {}: {}".format\(cat, samples\)\)

    # Verify new rows
    print\("\\n" + "=" * 55\)
    print\("NEW ROWS VERIFICATION"\)
    print\("=" * 55\)
    for row in rows:
        if row["source_id"] in \("SRC201", "SRC202"\):
            print\("  {} | {} | {} | {}".format\(
                row["source_id"], row["source_name"],
                row["scrape_category"], row["signal_quality"]\)\)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main\(\)
PYEOF)
